A.title: FINANCIALS
A.inline_verdict: Light pass — regulatory-mechanical trough; cash story is the tell

rows.label: Revenue (FY25)
rows.delta: broadly flat vs FY24 EUR 1,037m; operating rev EUR 1,173m

rows.label: EBITDA (FY25)
rows.delta: -19% YoY headline — __RP4 balance mechanism__ removal, not operational

rows.label: EPS (FY25, EUR)
rows.delta: depressed by accounting; FY26E recovery to ~0.19-0.20

rows.delta: __+32% YoY__ — cash tells the real story; beat EUR 240m target by 10%

blocks.text: The headline P&L is __misleading__. RP4 (the new 2025-2029 regulatory period) __eliminated quarterly balance smoothing__, so FY25 reported EBITDA (EUR 253m) and NI (EUR 93m) collapsed relative to FY24 (EUR 311m / EUR 126m) purely for accounting reasons. The real cash engine __accelerated__: FCF +32% to EUR 264m, net debt down EUR 121m in one year, FY25 NI beat the upgraded guidance range (EUR 78-83m) by 12-15%. Traffic service units grew +5.9% YoY (overflight +7.7%, intl +6.6%, domestic -1.2%) against a plan assumption of 2.5%.

Financials rate __B at Triaging__. The regulated monopoly cost-recovery model and 5-year RP4 certainty produce an __A on predictability__ and a __B on growth profile__ (4.3% plan CAGR is respectable for a utility). The FY25 headline optics are the main friction — sell-side consensus models have not yet fully rebuilt for RP4 accounting, creating __informational asymmetry__ that benefits prepared buyers. FCF quality is excellent; balance sheet is deleveraging; dividend coverage on FCF is comfortable (60% payout). Worth ESA depth to pressure-test H1-26 mechanical recovery.
_Guidance at ESA depth requires a three-way read: (i) internal conservatism vs external visibility, (ii) track record of delivery, (iii) re-setting triggers._

__FY26 guidance stack:__ FCF ~EUR 273m (~EUR 10m above FY25); 3% traffic growth assumption (vs FY25 +5.9%; Eurocontrol leading indicators tracking +4-5%); stable RP4 tariffs; progressive dividend EUR 0.30 (vs EUR 0.29 FY25). __2025-2029 strategic plan:__ EUR 1.2bn revenue (4.3% CAGR), EUR 361m EBITDA (~30% margin restored), EUR 165m NI, non-regulated >EUR 100m, capex EUR 570m (~EUR 114m/year). __Track record:__ FY24 beat top of upgraded range; FY25 beat upgrade by 12-15% on NI; under-delivered FY23 modestly when FCF trough mis-timed. __Internal conservatism: B+__ — 3% traffic vs +4-5% trending = ~150-200bps buffer. __External visibility: A__ — Eurocontrol leading indicators 2-3 quarters ahead; airline schedule data (IATA, Cirium) confirms +4-5% Q1-26 trajectory. __Resetting triggers:__ management has historically reset upward at H1 print (2 of last 3 years) — would expect similar if H1-26 EBITDA prints >EUR 130m.

_SS spread is modestly bullish on ratings, bearish on TPs — the asymmetry is the informational opportunity. Probability of positive EPS revisions over N0-6M: __70-75%__ if H1-26 EBITDA prints >EUR 120m._

__Consensus structure:__ 4 Buy (Mediobanca EUR 5.30, Equita EUR 5.20, Barclays EUR 5.90, UBS EUR 5.10) / 4-5 Hold (Intesa EUR 4.60, Kepler Cheuvreux EUR 4.80, Berenberg EUR 4.49) / 0-2 Sell. __Consensus TP cluster EUR 4.49-4.74__; high Barclays EUR 5.90; low EUR 3.50-3.70. __Stock at EUR 5.10 trades ABOVE consensus TP__ — informational asymmetry: prepared buyers ahead of model rebuild. __FY25 EPS 'miss' of -16.57%__ reported by Investing.com almost certainly reflects consensus models not yet adjusting for RP4 balance-mechanism removal — analyst-day notes from Mediobanca (Mar-26) explicitly flag model-rebuild in progress. __Revisions calendar:__ Q1-26 print 12-May-26 (seasonal loss; look past P&L to QoS accrual + traffic); H1-26 print Jul/Aug 2026 = the de-risking event; expect TP cluster lift to EUR 5.30-5.70 if H1-26 EBITDA >EUR 130m. __Short interest <0.5%__ (MEF 53.3% + low free float make activist shorts structurally absent).

_Modal case delivers 12-15% above current consensus EPS by FY28; supports re-rate to 18-20x forward EPS. Bear case is survivable thanks to the regulated cash-return floor — that's the asymmetric R/R foundation._

__Modal (p=60%):__ FY26E EUR 1,070m rev / 285m EBITDA / 105m NI / 0.19 EPS; FY27E EUR 1,110m / 310m / 125m / 0.23; FY28E EUR 1,150m / 335m / 145m / 0.27. Build: EBITDA margin recovers 24.7% FY25 → 26.7% FY26 → 27.9% FY27 → 29.1% FY28 (mix shift; balance true-up + non-reg + operating leverage). FY28 EPS EUR 0.27 × 22-23x forward P/E = __EUR 5.95-6.21 fair value__. Plus ~5% yield over 2yr ~ __25-35% TSR__. __Bull (p=25%):__ non-reg EUR 80m+ FY28 (vs EUR 60m base), traffic sustains +4%+, AIView accretion exceeds plan → EUR 0.30 EPS → 25x = __EUR 7.50__. __Bear (p=15%):__ traffic stalls +1-2%, RP4 mid-review tightens unit rates, capex overruns → revert to consensus EUR 0.21 EPS × 20x = __EUR 4.20__. __Probability-weighted fair value: EUR 5.80-6.10__ (≈ 14-20% upside vs current EUR 5.10).
_Modal ~23-25% TSR p.a. over 2-3y; bear ~10-12% p.a. R/R is asymmetric thanks to the regulated dividend floor. ESA sizing rule: 1.5-2.0% starter post-pullback; cap 2.5% until H1-26 prints clean._

__Modal decomposition (3Y CAGR):__ EPS growth +15% p.a. (FY25 EUR 0.17 trough → FY28 EUR 0.27) + multiple re-rate +3% p.a. (15-16x trough → 19-20x normalised) + dividend yield +5.5% p.a. = __~23-25% TSR p.a.__ __Bear decomposition:__ EPS growth +6% p.a. (recovery delayed), no re-rate, dividend yield 5.5%, FX 0% = __~10-12% TSR p.a.__ — still positive thanks to regulated cash-return floor. __Sequencing rule (APM SOP):__ 1.0-1.5% starter at ESA pass; +0.5-1.0% on first confirming H1-26 print (Jul/Aug 26); +0.5% on first M&A deal at claimed multiples (e.g. AIView accretion); cap 2.5% until DD P1 complete. Do NOT size >2.0% before H1-26 print. __Stop-loss: -15% vs entry OR ACH-trip__ (whichever first; #5 NT/MT one-two and #9 SS EEG are most likely first triggers given premium multiple).

B.title: EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
B.inline_verdict: Progress to ESA — regulated monopoly at mechanical earnings trough

blocks.text: __demibold__ESA pass — progress to DD P1.__ ENAV's core thesis hardens at ESA: __RP4 balance-mechanism removal is mechanically fading__ (FY26E EBITDA recovery EUR 280-300m vs FY25 trough EUR 253m), traffic momentum compounds the recovery (+5.9% FY25 vs +2.5% plan; Eurocontrol leading indicators support sustained +4-5% through H1-26), and the 12-month fulcrum event (H1-26 print Jul/Aug 26) sits inside our investment horizon. ESA delta vs Triaging: deeper SS read (4 Buy / 4-5 Hold; consensus TP cluster building toward EUR 5.10-5.30 post-FY25), confirmed unit-cost gap (EUR 490 ENAV vs EUR 470 ACE-best — slow-burn risk, not immediate), and non-reg credibility upgraded from C to B (IDS AirNav backlog visibility + AIView accretion). __Sizing: 1.5-2.0% starter at next pullback to EUR 4.85-5.00 zone OR on H1-26 print confirmation.__

__ESA findings overlaid on Triaging:__ (i) __Mechanical recovery now quantified__ — H1-26 EBITDA bridge (EUR 68.8m H1-25 → EUR 130-140m H1-26E; balance-true-up adds ~EUR 50-60m; traffic adds ~EUR 12-15m; cost discipline ~EUR 5m). FY26E EUR 280-300m EBITDA mechanically deliverable if traffic stays >+3%. (ii) __Unit-cost gap is real but bounded__ — Eurocontrol ACE 2024 confirms ENAV at EUR 490/composite-flight-hour (mid-tier; DFS EUR 425, NATS EUR 580, Skyguide EUR 540). RP5 (2030+) likely tightens efficiency factor by 10-15bps p.a. — slow burn, not thesis-breaker. (iii) __Non-regulated upgraded__ — IDS AirNav 2025 backlog EUR 220m+ (3 years revenue cover at run-rate); AIView FY26 accretion EUR 8-10m; D-Flight commercial UTM launch H2-26. __EUR 70-90m FY29 base case__ (vs EUR 100m mgmt target; 60-70% probability).

__ESA-active 10 INVALIDATION ACHs read:__ none tripped at ESA-pass. Watch items: __KR-1 (RP5 efficiency tightening)__ is a slow-burn KDIC-2/KR-1 axis — not invalidation-trigger at this stage; __KR-3 (premium multiple)__ — 28-32x trailing P/E vs Aena 18x is structurally defensible at the trough but creates mean-reversion vulnerability if H1-26 disappoints. __KR-2 (labour rigidity)__ ATCO union negotiations Q3-26 — contract renewal in line with regulatory efficiency factor is the base case; risk is asymmetric (no upside, downside if breached).

items.text: __Foundations rating moves Triaging A- → ESA A.__ Moat A+ confirmed; Fundamental Change A+ (mechanical + measurable + 12m fulcrum); Management upgrades B- → B (Monti track record now 3 years of conservative-setter delivery; board renewal political risk reduced post-AGM May-26 watch).
__Outputs rating moves Triaging B → ESA B+.__ Predictability A; Cash A; EGP B+ (recovery path now quantified); ROIC B (regulated WACC 5.65% vs ROIC 8-10%).

__Setup classification confirmed:__ Earnings-Recovery EPSU/EPT (primary; mechanical, high-probability); Quality-Compounder-with-Yield (secondary; structural moat + 5-6% yield floor + progressive policy).

__VF modal case at ESA:__ FY28E EUR 1,150m / EUR 335m / EUR 145m / EUR 0.27 EPS → fair value EUR 5.80-6.30 (modal) vs current EUR 5.10. Bull p=25%: EUR 6.60-7.00; Bear p=15%: EUR 4.50-4.70. __Asymmetry positive but not screaming__ — entry timing matters; pullback to EUR 4.85-5.00 transforms R/R.

blocks.text: Rollup: __Outputs balanced B-A__ (predictability and cash A; growth and ROIC B). __Foundations skewed A__ on moat/BtE and fulcrum clarity; management and governance B/B- as calibration points. Checks strong (Short, Incident, Accounting all A-B) apart from governance overhang from state ownership. Fit-for-fighting good on catalysts; liquidity a soft B from low true float. Setup __A__ — earnings-recovery EPSU/EPT is legibly present with measurable H1-26 catalyst. Rollup rating at Triaging: __B+/A-__ — above the Triaging bar, worth ESA depth.

Outputs at Triaging read as __B-composite__ — the mix of an A-rated predictability/cash pillar against B-rated growth/TSR reflects the reality that this is a __regulated-utility with recovery optionality__, not a compounder. FY25 depressed earnings will normalise mechanically into FY26-27; 3-year EPS CAGR of ~15% from the FY25 trough is plausible but not thrilling in absolute. What makes it worth ESA depth: the combination of __A-grade predictability + A-grade cash + legible 12-month catalyst (H1-26)__ at a price that sits above sell-side TP — i.e., an informational asymmetry, not a growth call.

items.text: If H1-26 EBITDA prints >EUR 120m (vs H1-25 EUR 69m), Outputs re-rate to __B+__ and the case progresses to DD P1. If H1-26 disappoints (<EUR 100m), Outputs drop to __B-__ and case parks until 9M-26. Either way, the H1 print is a __discrete go/no-go__ event — one of the cleaner Triaging-to-DD paths in current pipeline.

blocks.text: If H1-26 EBITDA prints >EUR 120m (vs H1-25 EUR 69m), Outputs re-rate to __B+__ and the case progresses to DD P1. If H1-26 disappoints (<EUR 100m), Outputs drop to __B-__ and case parks until 9M-26. Either way, the H1 print is a __discrete go/no-go__ event — one of the cleaner Triaging-to-DD paths in current pipeline.

__P3+P4 at Triaging__ — full canonical CQ → RA → TC structure. Fundamental Change pillar __A-rated__ (mechanical earnings recovery + traffic tailwind). Moat / Fit pillar __A+-rated__ (statutory monopoly + structural BtE). Management pillar __B-rated__ (CEO non-specialist; board renewal is a watch item). Net: Foundations __A-__ at Triaging.

__CQ3.1__: What is the specific, measurable change driving the investment case? __ENAV answer__: (a) RP4 balance mechanism removal creates __mechanical earnings trough in FY25 with recovery path FY26-29__; (b) Italian traffic growth __+5.9% FY25 vs 2.5% plan assumption__ — a 340bps tailwind that flows via risk-sharing to above-determined-cost revenue; (c) non-regulated revenue __+60% YoY__ toward EUR 100m FY29 target. __TC__: H1-26 EBITDA print >EUR 120m (vs H1-25 EUR 69m) confirms mechanical recovery; at-or-above plan FY29 EBITDA EUR 361m confirms full thesis.

__CQ3.2__: Why now vs 12 months ago? The __FY25 results (Mar-26)__ crystallised the RP4 accounting effect — consensus models are now being rebuilt but have not yet caught up. H1-26 print (Jul/Aug 2026) will be the first clean year-over-year comparison under RP4. __TC__: sell-side TP cluster should rise 15-25% (to EUR 5.30-5.70) in the 6 months post H1-26 if thesis plays out.

__CQ3.3__: What is the probability the change succeeds, and over what time frame? __Modal 60%__ over 12-24 months (mechanical accounting normalisation is high-probability if H1-26 confirms); bull 25% (traffic + non-reg upside); bear 15% (dividend holds but earnings recovery delayed 12 months). __TC__: aggregate ~85% probability of at-least-modal outcome over 24M.

__CQ3.4__: Is the change already priced? __No__ — stock trades EUR 5.10 vs sell-side TP cluster EUR 4.49-4.74. The __premium to TP reflects investors ahead of consensus__, not a fully priced re-rate. Actual re-rate would take multiple to 22-25x forward EPS (currently ~22-25x on depressed FY25 base; normalises to ~17-18x on FY28 EPS). __TC__: FY28 P/E compresses to <18x as EPS recovers = re-rate room.

__CQ4.1__: What is the competitive moat and is it widening/holding/narrowing? __ENAV moat is regulatory-statutory, not competitive__ — ICAO + EU law designate ENAV as the sole ANSP for Italian airspace. 100% market share by decree, entry legally impossible, EASA safety certification required, 1,700 trained ATCOs, nationwide radar/comms infrastructure. __Moat is structurally permanent__ at the Italian FIR level. __TC__: no meaningful SES2+ or pan-European ATM consolidation threat within 10-year investment horizon.

__CQ4.2__: What is the pricing power profile? __Regulated unit rates__ with cost recovery + risk-sharing mechanism (30% band at ±2% of plan). Unit cost ~EUR 490/composite flight-hour — mid-tier vs DFS Germany (lower) and NATS UK (higher). __Pricing not discretionary__; RP4 sets 2025-2029 envelope. __TC__: RP4 mid-term review (2027) does NOT reduce unit rates below 2025 levels in real terms.

__CQ4.3__: What is the capital intensity and ROIC profile? __Capex EUR 114m/year under RP4__ (SESAR CP1 deployment by 2027). ROIC ~8-10% against regulated WACC ~5.65% — structurally attractive. Non-regulated ROIC higher (20%+) but small base (EUR 30-52m). __TC__: ROIC stays >WACC through the full RP4 period.

__CQ4.4__: What's the position in the value chain? __Value-capturing node__ — sits between airlines (price-insensitive demand) and EC/ENAC rate-setters (regulatory protection). Captures the monopoly rent of Italian airspace. IDS AirNav subsidiary sells ATM systems globally (competitive, thin margin but high optionality). __TC__: value chain position stable; no disintermediation risk within 10yr.

__CQ4.5__: Is management aligned, competent, and capable of executing the change? __CEO Pasqualino Monti__ (appointed Apr-23, Meloni-slate, port-authority background) — __non-ATC specialist__ is a watch item. Track record in first 2 years: __consistent over-delivery__ against conservative guidance; debt-funded M&A signalled as new strategic pillar (step-change from historical organic bias). __TC__: board renewal at AGM (~May-26) does NOT materially alter strategic plan targets.

__CQ4.6__: Governance and state-ownership overhang? __MEF 53.3% stake__ creates political risk — Leonardo related-party procurement (MEF owns both). __Board term expires at AGM 2026__. This is the single largest governance consideration. __TC__: no sudden CEO change or strategic plan revision downward within 90 days of AGM.

__PoC summary at Triaging__: Foundations are A- (moat A+, change A, management B-). The management pillar is the binding constraint, not the moat or the change. Worth ESA depth to stress-test the board renewal outcome and the CEO's 3-year strategic plan execution record.

Checks __clear at Triaging depth__. No aggressive accounting flags (RP4 balance mechanism is regulatory, not management-driven). No activist short campaigns (SI <0.5%). No material safety incident history (0.014 min delay/flight vs 0.14 target — best-in-class EU punctuality). Governance __B-__ reflects state-ownership overhang (MEF 53.3%) and imminent board renewal — not a disqualifier but a watch item.

items.text: __Incident history__: no material safety incident or ATCO strike in last 5 years. __Governance__: state-controlled (MEF 53.3%); Leonardo related-party procurement; board renewal spring 2026. __Accounting__: IFRS, Big-4 audited; RP4 balance mechanism adds complexity but is fully disclosed in RP4 Implementing Decision 2025/1058 (May-25). __Legal__: no material litigation flagged. __Short interest__: <0.5%; structural reasons prevent activist shorts (low float, state ownership, regulated transparent revenue).

blocks.text: __Incident history__: no material safety incident or ATCO strike in last 5 years. __Governance__: state-controlled (MEF 53.3%); Leonardo related-party procurement; board renewal spring 2026. __Accounting__: IFRS, Big-4 audited; RP4 balance mechanism adds complexity but is fully disclosed in RP4 Implementing Decision 2025/1058 (May-25). __Legal__: no material litigation flagged. __Short interest__: <0.5%; structural reasons prevent activist shorts (low float, state ownership, regulated transparent revenue).

Catalyst density is __high__: Q1-26 results (12-May-26), AGM + FY25 dividend approval (14-May-26), ex-div (23-Jun-26), __H1-26 results Jul/Aug 2026 — the key de-risking event__, 9M-26 (Nov-26), FY26 + strategic plan Year-2 (Mar-27). Plus macro catalysts: SES2+ Member-State implementation deadline Dec-26, RP4 mid-period review 2027. Squad fit: complements DD RO heavyweights (NVTK, HTRO) with regulated-utility diversification and dividend income.

blocks.title: P5 SS Earnings Momentum — 5 dimensions x 3 timeframes (ENAV consensus snapshot)

items.text: Liquidity is the friction — EUR 2.72bn market cap is comfortable on paper but ~24% true public float (MEF 53.3% + institutional 22.6%) means position sizing has a soft ceiling at ~2% of portfolio without slippage concerns. For 1.0-1.5% starter size, liquidity is fine. For 2.5-3% target, H1-26 de-risking is prerequisite.

blocks.text: __Richard gate question__: is EUR 2.72bn market cap with ~24% true float a fit for the concentrated-5-to-15-positions portfolio? At starter 1.0-1.5% size, yes — liquidity comfortable. Escalate to Richard if sizing ambitions exceed 2.0%; the thin float argues for a slower scaling path than liquid mega-caps.

__Classification__: __Earnings-Recovery EPSU/EPT (primary)__ — mechanical RP4 accounting normalisation over 12-18 months + structural Italian traffic tailwind. __Quality-Compounder with Yield (secondary)__ — statutory monopoly + 5-6% dividend floor + progressive dividend policy. __Time horizon__: 24-36 months for full case; 12 months to first meaningful re-rate; H1-26 print (Jul/Aug 2026) is the fulcrum event. __Conviction at Triaging__: C (forming) — worth ESA depth.

D.title: COMPONENTS
D.inline_verdict: KDICs legible; 10 INVALIDATION ACHs armed at Triaging

subsections.title: KDICs — Key Drivers of Investment Case

items.text: __demibold__KDIC-1 — Balance mechanism normalises, reported EBITDA recovers to EUR 280m+ by FY26__. Measurable: H1-26 EBITDA __>EUR 120m__ (vs H1-25 EUR 68.8m); FY26 EBITDA EUR 270-290m. Why matters: the -19% FY25 EBITDA decline is accounting, not operational. H1-26 print is the first clean YoY comparison under RP4. Window: N6-12M.

__demibold__KDIC-2 — Traffic growth remains ≥3% in FY26 (above the 2.5% plan assumption)__. Measurable: en-route service units __+3% minimum__ in FY26 (vs +5.9% FY25). Eurocontrol monthly Italian FIR data is the leading indicator. Milan-Cortina Winter Olympics 2026 provides additional impulse. Window: N0-12M.

__demibold__KDIC-3 — Iran airspace closure persists, adds 1-3% incremental Italian overflight service units__. Measurable: Italian overflight SU growth __>5%__ in FY26 (tracking above domestic +7.7% FY25). FAA NOTAM extensions on Iranian/Iraqi FIR. Russian precedent (4+yr closed) suggests semi-permanent rerouting. Window: N6-24M.

__KDIC-4 — Dividend delivery on EUR 0.30 FY26 and progressive path to EUR 0.32 FY29__. Measurable: AGM 14-May-26 approves EUR 0.29 FY25; FY26 board proposal EUR 0.30 at FY26 results (Mar-27). Payout 100% on reported EPS but only 60% on FCF — sustainable while FCF holds. Window: N0-24M.

__KDIC-5 — Non-regulated revenue reaches EUR 60m+ FY27 en route to EUR 100m FY29__. Measurable: non-regulated line disclosure in H1-26 and FY26 shows __≥30% YoY growth__. AIView integration completes Q1-26. Two-three IDS AirNav large contract wins. Window: N12-24M.
__KHF-1__ — RP4 regulatory framework (2025-2029) remains stable; no mid-period review (2027) reduces unit rates below 2025 levels in real terms.
__KHF-2__ — Italian ATCO labour contract stays within efficiency-factor envelope; no strike action; no pay award materially above CPI+2pp.
__KHF-3__ — MEF retains state ownership; no sudden privatisation or political interference with management; board renewal spring 2026 does not disrupt strategic continuity.

__KC-1__ — What is the H1-26 balance adjustment shape, and is FY26 EUR 280m+ EBITDA mechanically deliverable? Central analytical question. DD P1 action: build detailed balance mechanism model from RP4 Implementing Decision text + ENAV disclosures.

__KC-2__ — What is the real unit-economics gap between ENAV and the European top-performer? Sources disagree on cost/composite-flight-hour (EUR 470 ACE vs ENAV-specific EUR 490). DD P1 action: Eurocontrol ACE 2024/2025 reports, compare vs DFS, NATS, Skyguide.

__KC-3__ — Is the >EUR 100m non-regulated revenue target genuinely in reach, or a narrative device? Current base EUR 30-52m (sources disagree). Target implies ~35% CAGR in a competitive market (Thales, Indra, Frequentis). DD P1 action: segmental disclosure review, IDS AirNav customer list, AIView integration plan.

__KR-1 — Regulatory squeeze via SES2+ / RP5__. RP5 (2030+) likely tighter as PRB enforces convergence. ENAV unit cost mid-tier — efficiency factor could be tougher next period. Slow-burn margin compression risk.

__KR-2 — Labour cost rigidity__. 80% of opex is personnel (EUR 592m FY24). Italian employment law + union power means ATCO headcount can't be reduced without strike risk. Wage awards historically above regulatory efficiency factor.

__KR-3 — Valuation decoupled from regulated-peer fundamentals__. 28-32x trailing P/E is 50-80% premium to Aena (18x), Fraport (14-16x), Vinci (14x). EV/EBITDA looks cheap at 7x but FY25 EBITDA is depressed. Premium not entirely defensible outside the trough.

__KPO-1 — Iran airspace closure extends or expands__. Russian precedent (4+yr) suggests semi-permanent rerouting; potential second-order option if Middle East instability widens. Adds 1-3% incremental Italian overflight SU.

__KPO-2 — Non-regulated exceeds EUR 100m FY29 target__. IDS AirNav customer wins (Nepal, Malaysia, Swiss ANSP), D-Flight drone UTM scaling, AIView accretive — each small but compound to meaningful re-rate lever.

__KPO-3 — Dividend progressive path exceeds plan (beyond EUR 0.32 FY29)__. FCF trajectory supports higher. Progressive-dividend re-rating in European utility space is well-established; each 1c of DPS above consensus adds ~0.2x multiple.

blocks.text: _{10_INVALIDATION_ACHS} __Live-case invalidation discipline.__ Applies while we own a case at every stage including Triaging. One-strike rules — APM escalates immediately on trip. Locked 22-Apr-26; see feedback_live_case_invalidations.md._
__Single-event invalidations (rules 1-5)__ — fundamental triggers independent of share price action.

items.text: **1. Top-line invalidation** — One __probable or actual near-term revenue cut__ to SS/G caused by an exogenous problem.
**2. Cockroach invalidation** — __One actual AND one probable__, OR __three probable/likely__ current/near-term problems (cause internal or exogenous; impact on profits or revenue).
**3. Ditherer invalidation** — __Deterioration in operator assessment__ AND one or more current/near-term probable/actual problems (cause internal or exogenous; impact on profits or revenue).
**4. Cyclical invalidation** — __SP underperformance of 15% or 3M__ FOLLOWED BY a __plausible+ near-term revenue cut__ caused by a threshold+ exogenous problem.
**5. NT/MT one-two invalidation** — __SP underperformance of 15% or 3M__ FOLLOWED BY any __probable/actual near-term cut__ to profits (revenue is covered by #4).

blocks.text: __SP-conditional & composite invalidations (rules 6-10)__ — share-price underperformance must follow or co-occur with fundamental signal.

items.text: **6. Wisdom of crowd invalidation** — __SP underperformance of 15% or 3M__ FOLLOWED BY any plausible+ VF or actual SM threshold+ concerns re. __mid/long-term growth, margins, SRCAs__ or predictability thereof.

**7. Market catch-up with our existential concerns invalidation** — __VF (not SM) plausible+ threshold+ concerns__ re. mid/long-term growth, margins, SRCAs or predictability thereof FOLLOWED BY __SP underperformance of 15% or 3M__.
**8. Narrow frame invalidation** — __Peerset underperformance of 15% or 3M__ FOLLOWED BY any __actual cut/problem__ (cause internal or exogenous; impact on profits or revenue).
**9. SS EEG invalidation** — __[2]% or greater SS EPS cuts__ AND __SP underperformance of 15% or 3M__ (either order).
**10. Case outputs/attributes invalidation** — __[8] or more case-output thresholds at D or F.__

blocks.text: _Escalation protocol: when a threshold trips, APM (i) records the event, (ii) notifies Richard immediately with sell/trim recommendation, (iii) does NOT wait for the 30-day shot clock. Shot clock is for deterioration without a tripped threshold._

Beyond the 10 standard ACHs, ENAV-specific exit triggers per DD depth: (i) __H1-26 EBITDA prints <EUR 100m__ (base: EUR 120m+) — mechanical recovery impaired; (ii) __RP4 mid-term review (2027) reduces unit rates__ below 2025 levels in real terms; (iii) __FY26 dividend cut below EUR 0.28__ (vs FY25 EUR 0.29 + progressive policy); (iv) __CEO departure without successor plan__ at or around board renewal; (v) __non-regulated revenue flat or declining__ for two consecutive halves (target: +30% YoY).

subsections.title: Negative, should-be-expected developments — Watson note on coverage gaps

blocks.text: __ESA-active thesis challenges to test (not yet tripped):__ (i) __RP5 efficiency-factor tightening__ — PRB convergence pressure suggests RP5 (2030+) unit-cost glide path -10 to -15bps p.a. ENAV mid-tier (EUR 490 vs ACE-best EUR 425) means efficiency factor likely tougher; not a Triaging/ESA thesis breaker but a slow-burn DD-RO concern. (ii) __Premium multiple mean-reversion__ — 28-32x trailing P/E sits 50-80% above European airport/infra peers (Aena 18x, Fraport 14-16x, Vinci 14x). Multiple defensible at the trough but vulnerable to any execution stumble. Watch for sentiment cooling if H1-26 disappoints. (iii) __ATCO union negotiations Q3-26__ — wage settlement above efficiency factor would compress margin; historically Italian ATCO awards run 100-150bps above CPI. Both are watchlist items requiring RESEARCHER 06-SS + 09-IR + 12-WC follow-through.

__Negative developments to be expected (do not constitute thesis breaks):__ Q1-26 seasonal operating loss is normal — look past P&L to traffic (Eurocontrol), QoS bonus accrual, FCF shape. 1-2 quarters of traffic moderating from +5.9% toward +3-4% is consistent with mean-reversion + plan. Occasional negative RP4-implementation noise as quarterly tariff mechanics settle is normal. Short-seller activity is structurally absent (MEF 53.3% + <0.5% SI). Premium-multiple compression of 5-10% on any post-print sentiment cooling is consistent with thesis intact.

__ESA coverage status:__ RESEARCHER 06-SS (Sell-Side deep-dive), 09-IR (IR meeting prep), 12-WC (Wider Context / thematics) queued for DD P1 prep. KA-4 (Q14 Key Drivers Assessment) recommended next — explicit canonical ESA→DD-P1 step; ties KDIC-1 (mechanical recovery) and KDIC-2 (traffic) to specific measurable conditions over N6-18M. Pullback monitor for ENAV.MI must be activated at DD P1 kickoff (sector misclassification fix in pullback-watchlist.json: Telecoms → Transport Infrastructure / ATM).

items.text: __KOQ-1__ — What is the precise H1-26 balance-effect trajectory? Need RP4 Implementing Decision 2025/1058 text deep-read + ENAV disclosures to build bridge model.
__KOQ-2__ — Who are the post-AGM-2026 board members, and does CEO Monti get renewed? Watch for political interference signal.
__KOQ-3__ — What is the real non-regulated revenue base (sources disagree EUR 30-52m) and what are the 2-3 largest IDS AirNav customer contracts?
__KA-1__ — Run pullback monitor for ENAV.MI + fix sector classification in pullback-watchlist.json (Telecoms → Transport Infrastructure/ATM). Deadline: this week.
__KA-2__ — Queue RESEARCHER 06-SS (Sell-Side Analysis deep-dive) + 09-IR (IR meeting prep) + 12-WC (Wider Context / thematics) before ESA depth. Deadline: next 5 working days.
__KA-3__ — Build RP4 balance-mechanism earnings-bridge model (FY25 trough → FY29 plan target). Stress-test H1-26 scenarios. Deadline: ESA+0 (start of ESA).

__KA-4 — Run RESEARCHER Q14 Key Drivers Assessment (C+AS) at DD P1 kickoff__. Explicit next step per master data; ties KDIC-1 (RP4 balance mechanical recovery) and KDIC-2 (traffic >3%) to measurable conditions over N6-18M.

__KA-5 — Build RP4 balance-mechanism bridge model at DD P1 kickoff__. H1-25 EUR 68.8m EBITDA → H1-26E EUR 130-140m. Decompose: balance true-up, traffic delta, cost discipline. Stress-test ±EUR 20m H1-26 outcome.
__KA-6 — Schedule IR call (09-IR) before H1-26 print__. Targets: Eurocontrol Q2-26 traffic confirm, ATCO union negotiation update, AIView integration milestone, IDS AirNav backlog progression.

subsections.title: Pros/cons of prioritising vs other opportunities

blocks.text: __Progress to ESA.__ Setup is legible (Earnings-Recovery EPSU/EPT + Quality-Compounder-with-Yield). Moat is A+ (statutory monopoly). Change catalyst is mechanical (RP4 accounting normalisation) with a discrete 12-month fulcrum event (H1-26 print Jul/Aug 2026). Foundations A-; Outputs B. Informational asymmetry exists (stock above sell-side TP; models being rebuilt for RP4). 10 INVALIDATION ACHs armed — no triggers near threshold at entry. Size at Triaging: 1.0-1.5% starter pending ESA.
If H1-26 prints <EUR 100m EBITDA — park case until 9M-26 print. If CEO replaced at AGM with downward strategic plan revision within 90 days, exit.
Lorem ipsum — monitoring plan placeholder. Define tracking indicators, investment case drivers, and peer group for ongoing RESEARCHER monitoring. Specify frequency and escalation triggers.

subsections.title: KQs/KAs if/when progressed to DD P1

items.text: Progress to __ESA depth__ immediately — run Q14 Key Drivers Assessment (canonical ESA step) + SS deep-dive + value chain + business model primer in parallel.
Start __RP4 balance-mechanism bridge model__ at ESA kickoff — the H1-26 recovery math is the central analytical question.
Queue a __1.0-1.5% starter__ at current EUR 5.10 IF pullback monitor confirms 8/8 Minervini + 50D hold; otherwise wait for pullback to EUR 4.80-5.00 zone.
Park with 9M-26 review trigger (Nov-26). 30-Day Shot Clock does NOT apply pre-entry; only post-entry.
Maintain pullback-monitor + Eurocontrol traffic dashboard watch for re-evaluation trigger — specifically H1-26 EBITDA >EUR 120m or traffic sustained +4%+.
